nickel.name: Koniambo Nickel SAS (KNS)
nickel.production: Transitioned to care and maintenance February 2024; Glencore 49% attributable production in 2023: 22,200 t Ni; 100% basis ~45,000 t Ni/yr when operating
nickel.description: Open-pit laterite mine and ferronickel smelter in New Caledonia's Northern Province, 49% owned by Glencore and 51% by SOFINOR (New Caledonian interests). Placed into care and maintenance in February 2024 due to high operating costs and weak nickel prices. Glencore failed to find a buyer; redundancy process for ~1,200 workers commenced August 2024. Plant in suspension since August 2024.
nickel.source: Glencore Full Year 2024 Production Report, January 2025 (glencore.com); Mining Data Online — Koniambo Mine profile, May 2025; Reuters, February 2024

nickel.name: Mining Industry Indonesia (MIND ID) — Pakal Island Mine
nickel.production: Pakal Island Mine: ~35,970 t Ni (2023, GlobalData estimate)
nickel.description: MIND ID is the Indonesian state mining holding company (Inalum/BUMN) that acts as parent of ANTAM, PT Vale Indonesia stake, and other state assets. The Pakal Island mine in North Maluku is listed separately as the 10th largest nickel mine globally per GlobalData's 2023 data, operated as part of the Indonesian industrial park ecosystem.
nickel.source: Mining Technology — World's ten largest nickel mines (mining-technology.com, 2023 GlobalData figures); Mining Technology — Indonesia nickel capacity additions 2026, February 2026

nickel.name: Korea Zinc Co., Ltd. / KEMCO
nickel.hq: Seoul, South Korea (refinery: Ulsan, Onsan National Industrial Complex)
nickel.production: Current capacity: ~22,000 t Ni/yr; all-in-one nickel refinery (KEMCO) under construction targeting 42,600 t Ni/yr additional capacity (total 65,000 t/yr) for commercial production in 2026
nickel.description: World's largest zinc smelter company and a growing nickel refiner/producer via its subsidiary KEMCO. Currently operates nickel refining at the Onsan refinery in Ulsan. A KRW 506.3 billion all-in-one nickel refinery capable of processing nickel matte, MHP and other intermediates into high-purity nickel sulphate broke ground November 2023 and targets 65,000 t Ni/yr by 2026 — which would make Korea Zinc/KEMCO the world's largest non-Chinese nickel sulphate producer.
nickel.source: Korea Zinc press release, November 2023 (koreazinc.co.kr); Wikipedia — Korea Zinc; Mining Technology, January 2025

nickel.name: Anglo American Platinum (Amplats) — Nickel By-product
nickel.production: ~25,700 t nickel (2024, as PGM by-product from South African Bushveld Complex operations)
nickel.description: The world's largest platinum group metals producer, operating underground mines in South Africa's Bushveld Igneous Complex. Nickel is a significant by-product of PGM smelting and refining. Anglo American Platinum's nickel production is separate from Anglo American's ferronickel operations in Brazil. JSE-listed (AMS) and LSE-listed.
nickel.source: Anglo American Q4 2024 Production Report footnote 6/7 (angloamerican.com, February 2025); LSE RNS disclosure February 2025 showing 25.7 kt PGM-sourced Ni in 2024

nickel.name: Ivanhoe Mines Ltd. — Platreef Project
nickel.hq: Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada (project: Mokopane, Limpopo, South Africa)
nickel.production: Not yet in production; Phase 1 shaft sinking complete; Phase 1 targeted start 2025–2026; projected annual nickel production not separately quantified (nickel is one of several co-products with PGMs, copper, gold)
nickel.description: TSX-listed Ivanhoe Mines is developing the Platreef underground mine in Limpopo, South Africa — the world's widest and flattest PGM-nickel-copper-gold deposit. Expected to be one of the world's largest low-cost PGM operations with 32-year mine life; nickel is a significant co-product. Mine NRI Digital ranked this as an upcoming major project in 2024.
nickel.source: Mine NRI Digital, April 2024 (mine.nridigital.com); IISD Commodity Profile Nickel 2025 (upcoming mines section)

nickel.name: Centaurus Metals Limited — Jaguar Nickel Project
nickel.hq: Perth, Western Australia, Australia (project: Pará state, Brazil)
nickel.production: Not yet in production; 15-year open-pit plan targeting ~20,000 t Ni/yr (average 22,600 t Ni/yr in first 7 years); FID targeted before end of September 2026; first production targeted early 2029
nickel.description: ASX-listed (CTM) developer of the Jaguar nickel sulphide project in Pará state, northern Brazil. Signed a binding 5-year offtake agreement with Glencore in March 2026 for 20,000 t/yr of 32% nickel concentrate (worth ~$450M). Received final environmental approval in October 2025. Capital cost estimated at $380M; in discussions with BNDES for debt financing.
nickel.source: Mining.com, March 2026 (centaurus-inks-glencore-deal); Newsfile Corp press release, March 2026; YouTube CEO Clips, March 2026

nickel.name: Horizonte Minerals plc — Araguaia Nickel Project
nickel.hq: London, United Kingdom (project: Pará/Tocantins border, Brazil)
nickel.production: Project entered care and maintenance 2024 due to financing difficulties; designed capacity: 29,000 t Ni/yr (ferronickel); mine life 28 years
nickel.description: AIM- and TSX-listed developer of the Araguaia Nickel Ferronickel Project in Brazil, targeting first metal for Q1 2024 before encountering financing difficulties. The project — which acquired Vale's former Araguaia exploration assets — entered care and maintenance in 2024. It holds one of the largest undeveloped nickel reserves in Brazil.
nickel.source: Mine NRI Digital, April 2024 (mine.nridigital.com, Brazil section); company public disclosures (Horizonte Minerals entered significant financial difficulty during 2024)

nickel.name: Solway Investment Group — Pobuzhsky Ferronickel Plant (PFP), Ukraine
nickel.hq: Zug, Switzerland (plant: Pobuzhskoye, Kirovohrad Oblast, Ukraine)
nickel.production: Halted October 2022 (Russian missile strike destroyed power substation); capacity was >22,000 t Ni/yr and >1,100 kt dry laterite ore/yr
nickel.description: The Pobuzhsky Ferronickel Plant operated by Solway was the only ferronickel smelter in Ukraine and a significant European nickel producer before being forcibly halted in October 2022 when a Russian missile strike destroyed the plant's power substation. The facility imported laterite ore from Indonesia, New Caledonia, and Guatemala. Included as a historically significant producer currently inactive.
nickel.source: Solway Group website (solwaygroup.com); Wikipedia — Solway Group; Mining.com / Energy News, January 2026

nickel.name: Nickel 28 Capital Corp. — Ramu JV Interest
nickel.country: Canada (interest in Papua New Guinea operation)
nickel.production: 8.56% JV interest in Ramu operation (MCC operates); attributable nickel: ~2,877 t (8.56% of 33,604 t in 2023)
nickel.description: TSX-V listed nickel-cobalt royalty and streaming company holding an 8.56% JV interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea (operated by MCC). Nickel 28 also holds a portfolio of 10 nickel and cobalt royalties on development and exploration projects in Canada, Australia, and Papua New Guinea. Included here as a direct equity participant in a significant producing operation.
nickel.source: Nickel 28 website (nickel28.com/about-us/company-profile/); Nickel 28 MDA January 2024; Business Wire, February 2024

nickel.name: Coral Bay Nickel Corporation (CBNC)
nickel.production: ~24,000 t Ni/yr (nameplate capacity ~22,000 t Ni/yr after 2009 expansion; actual production reached 24 kt/yr by 2014); Sumitomo 100% shareholder as of planned acquisition from Nickel Asia (15.625% stake sale, announced January 2025)
nickel.description: Operates the Philippines' first HPAL plant at Rio Tuba, Palawan, converting low-grade laterite ore from the adjacent Rio Tuba Nickel mine (Nickel Asia) into mixed nickel-cobalt sulphide (MS) for export to Sumitomo Metal Mining's refineries in Japan. Commissioned 2005, expanded 2009. Sumitomo Metal Mining is the majority shareholder (100% targeted); Nickel Asia is selling its remaining 15.625% stake.
nickel.source: Coral Bay Nickel Corporation website (cbnc.com.ph); Sumitomo Metal Mining sustainability page (smm.co.jp/en/sustainability); Mining Technology, January 2025; Wood Mackenzie CBNC report overview

nickel.name: Taganito HPAL Nickel Corporation (THPAL)
nickel.production: ~36,000 t Ni/yr (nameplate capacity, achieved from 2017 after expansion; started commercial operations 2013 at 30,000 t/yr)
nickel.description: HPAL plant in Taganito, Surigao del Norte, Philippines, processing laterite ore into mixed nickel-cobalt sulphide (MS) for export to Sumitomo Metal Mining's Japanese refineries. Owned 75% by Sumitomo Metal Mining, 15% by Mitsui & Co., and 10% by Nickel Asia Corporation. Commenced commercial production October 2013; capacity expanded to 36,000 t Ni/yr by FY2017.
nickel.source: Sumitomo Metal Mining sustainability page (smm.co.jp/en/sustainability/activity_highlights/article_12/); OneMine — THPAL Plant Operation Progress (2017); Mining.com, January 2025

nickel.name: Cerro Matoso S.A. (South32 — being sold to CoreX Holding)
nickel.production: 40,600 t payable nickel (FY2024, 100% of guidance achieved); 2025 guidance: 35,000 t (lower planned grades); 2023: 85,336,638 lbs nickel (royalty basis, Colombian ANM data)
nickel.description: Open-cut laterite mine and ferronickel smelter in Córdoba Department, Colombia. One of the largest open-pit ferronickel mines in the world and the only nickel mine in Colombia. Operated since 1980; owned by South32 since 2015. South32 entered a binding agreement to sell Cerro Matoso to CoreX Holding (July 2025) as part of its strategic shift toward copper and zinc.
nickel.source: South32 Quarterly Report June 2024 (south32.net); Colombian ANM Nickel Fact Sheet December 2024 (mineriaencolombia.anm.gov.co); Mining.com, July 2025 (South32 sale announcement); Cerro Matoso Wikipedia

nickel.name: Lundin Mining Corporation — Neves-Corvo (Nickel by-product)
nickel.production: Small nickel production as by-product from the Neves-Corvo copper-zinc-tin mine in Portugal (figures not separately reported; copper dominant product)
nickel.description: Lundin Mining's Neves-Corvo mine in Alentejo, southern Portugal produces copper, zinc, and tin with nickel as a minor by-product. One of the few European copper mines with nickel. Included here as a European nickel-producing mine, though nickel is a secondary product. TSX- and Nasdaq Stockholm-listed.
nickel.source: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries 2025 — Nickel (pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs2025/mcs2025-nickel.pdf) reference to other country producers; Lundin Mining annual report

manganese.production: Manganese ore sales volumes: 4.3 million metric tonnes in F2023 (year ended 30 June 2023), up 9% from F2022 (3.96 million tonnes). Export sales: 3.6 million tonnes; local sales: 0.73 million tonnes. Source: ARM F2023 Annual Results Commentary.

manganese.name: Tshipi é Ntle Manganese Mining (Pty) Ltd. — Tshipi Borwa Mine
manganese.production: 3.3 million metric tonnes of manganese ore produced in calendar year 2023 (FY2023 for owner OM Holdings: 3.3 Mt). Tshipi is on track to achieve 3.4 Mtpa per the Q3 FY2023 quarterly report (OM Holdings, ASX). Source: Mining Data Online / OM Holdings quarterly reports.

manganese.name: Grupo Industrial Camargo Corrêa / Mineração Pedra Branca do Amapari (Brazil)
manganese.production: Information not available — Brazil national total: 624 thousand metric tons Mn content (2022), 620 thousand metric tons Mn content (2023e), USGS MCS 2024.

manganese.name: Guyana Manganese Inc. (wholly owned subsidiary of Bosai Group, China)
manganese.production: 315,000 metric tonnes of manganese ore (gross weight) produced in 2023, at the Matthews Ridge Mine in northwest Guyana. Mine restarted in 2022 after nearly 54 years of dormancy. Source: USGS Minerals Yearbook 2023 — Manganese chapter.

manganese.name: Consolidated Minerals (Consmin) — Woodie Woodie Mine
manganese.production: Information not available — Australia national total: 3,040 thousand metric tons Mn content (2022), 3,000 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. Woodie Woodie is one of Australia's principal manganese operations alongside GEMCO.

manganese.name: Comilog / Société Minière de la Moanda (Gabon — Eramet subsidiary)
manganese.production: See Eramet entry above — Comilog is the operating entity for the Moanda mine in Gabon, producing 7.4 million metric tonnes of manganese ore in 2023.

manganese.name: Ghana Manganese Company Ltd. (GMC)
manganese.production: Information not available — Ghana national total: 844 thousand metric tons Mn content (2022), 840 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024.

manganese.name: MOIL Limited (Manganese Ore India Limited)
manganese.production: Information not available — India national total: 721 thousand metric tons Mn content (2022), 720 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. MOIL is the dominant Indian manganese producer.

manganese.name: JSC Nikopol Ferroalloy Plant (Ferrexpo / Privat Group related — Ukraine)
manganese.production: Information not available — Ukraine national total (concentrate basis): 323 thousand metric tons (2022), 320 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. JSC Nikopol Ferroalloy Plant temporarily suspended operations in November 2023 due to damage from Russian artillery (per USGS Minerals Yearbook 2023 — Manganese chapter).

chromium.name: Glencore plc — Merafe Chrome Venture
chromium.production: Attributable ferrochrome production: 1,162,000 metric tonnes in full year 2023 (79.85% Glencore share of the Glencore-Merafe venture), down 22% from 1,488,000 tonnes in 2022; reduction mainly due to planned additional smelter offline days during high electricity demand winter season. Source: Glencore Full Year 2023 Production Report, February 1, 2024.

chromium.name: Merafe Resources Ltd.
chromium.production: Attributable ferrochrome production: 300,000 metric tonnes for year ended 31 December 2023 (2022: 384,000 tonnes), down 22%. Merafe holds a 20.15% stake in the Glencore-Merafe Chrome Venture. Source: Merafe Resources Integrated Annual Report 2023.

chromium.name: Samancor Chrome (Pty) Ltd.
chromium.production: Information not available — Samancor Chrome is a privately held company (majority shareholder Terris Chrome with Japanese consortium and BEE shareholders). Specific annual production figures are not publicly disclosed. South Africa national total: 19,100 thousand metric tons chromite ore gross weight (2022), 18,000 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024.

chromium.name: TNC Kazchrome JSC (Eurasian Resources Group / ERG)
chromium.production: Record chrome ore output and ferroalloys production in 2024 (ERG/Kazchrome press release, June 2025). In 2024, Donskoy GOK reported 6 million tonnes of ore and 1.864 million tonnes of ferroalloys at record output. 2023 figures: Kazakhstan national total: 6,000 thousand metric tons chromite ore gross weight (2022e), 6,000 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. Kazchrome is the world's largest high-carbon ferrochrome producer by chrome content per ERG.

chromium.name: Outokumpu Oyj — Kemi Chromite Mine
chromium.production: Chromite ore: 1,906,409 metric tonnes produced in 2023; Ferrochromium: 390,000 metric tonnes produced in 2023. Source: USGS Minerals Yearbook 2023 — Chromium chapter.

chromium.name: Tata Steel Mining Limited (TSML) — Sukinda Chromite Mines
chromium.production: Ferrochrome production: 373,000 metric tonnes in 2021-22 (scaled up from 37,000 tonnes in 2020-21). 2022-23 and 2023-24 figures: India national total: 4,000 thousand metric tons chromite ore (2022e), 4,200 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. TSML's exact 2023 company-level production not separately stated.

chromium.name: Indian Metals & Ferro Alloys Ltd. (IMFA)
chromium.production: Targeting 800,000 metric tonnes of chrome ore production from Sukinda mines in the current fiscal year (2025-26) per IMFA corporate communications (August 2025). 2023 exact production not stated. India national total: 4,000 thousand metric tons chromite ore (2022e), 4,200 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024.

chromium.name: Odisha Mining Corporation Ltd. (OMC)
chromium.production: 0.37 million metric tonnes of chrome ore in Q1 FY2025-26 (Odisha Mining Corporation LinkedIn post, July 2025). 2023 exact production not stated. India national total: 4,000 thousand metric tons chromite ore (2022e), 4,200 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024.

chromium.name: Vedanta Resources Ltd. — Chromite (Odisha, India)
chromium.production: Information not available — Vedanta's ferrochrome capacity expansion plans: from 140,000 t/yr to 450,000 t/yr (per USGS Minerals Yearbook 2023 — Chromium). 2023 production not publicly stated.

chromium.name: Zimasco (Pvt) Ltd. (majority owned by Sinosteel, China)
chromium.production: Information not available — Zimasco's Kwekwe smelter restarted after a 2-month shutdown in 2023 per USGS Minerals Yearbook 2023 — Chromium. Zimbabwe is included in 'Other countries' in USGS MCS 2024 country totals.

chromium.name: Jubilee Metals Group Plc — Chromite Recovery (South Africa)
chromium.production: Information not available — Jubilee Metals operates a chromite concentrate recovery facility on the Bushveld Complex with capacity of 360,000 tonnes per year. 2023 production figures not stated in available sources.

chromium.name: Yildirim Group / American Chrome & Chemicals Inc.
chromium.production: Information not available — American Chrome & Chemicals (subsidiary) produces sodium dichromate from chromite ore at Castle Hayne, NC and Corpus Christi, TX. Turkey national total: 5,410 thousand metric tons chromite ore (2022), 6,000 thousand metric tons (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. Turkey-origin chromite production figures at Yildirim's own mines not separately disclosed.

titanium.name: PJSC VSMPO-AVISMA Corporation
titanium.production: Russia national sponge metal production: 20,000 metric tonnes (2022), 20,000 metric tonnes (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. VSMPO-AVISMA accounts for the majority of Russia's titanium sponge output. Company-level annual production figure: over 20,000 tonnes of finished titanium products per year per corporate website. Exact 2023 production not separately disclosed.

titanium.name: Osaka Titanium Technologies Co., Ltd. (OTC)
titanium.production: Information not available — OTC operated its Amagasaki plant 'at almost full capacity throughout 2023-24 to produce titanium sponge' (Argus Media, May 2024). Japan national sponge production: 47,000 metric tonnes (2022), 60,000 metric tonnes (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. OTC's capacity was 40,000 t/yr as of 2023 (expanding to 50,000 t/yr). OTC is described as the world's second largest titanium sponge producer after VSMPO-AVISMA.

titanium.name: Toho Titanium Co., Ltd.
titanium.production: Information not available — Toho Titanium is the world's third-largest titanium sponge producer per company's Medium-Term Management Plan FY2023-2025. Production capacity: domestic plants (Wakamatsu/Chigasaki) plus ATTM joint venture in Saudi Arabia (15,600 t/yr capacity). Japan national sponge: 47,000 metric tonnes (2022), 60,000 metric tonnes (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. Exact 2023 output not disclosed.

titanium.name: AMIC Toho Titanium Metal Co., Ltd. (ATTM) — Advanced Metal Industries Cluster
titanium.production: 12,000 metric tonnes of titanium sponge produced in 2023 (up from 9,700 t in 2022 and 5,700 t in 2021). Saudi Arabia national total: 12,000 metric tonnes (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. ATTM is a joint venture: AMIC (65%) and Toho Titanium (35%); plant capacity 15,600 t/yr. Source: USGS Mineral Industry of Saudi Arabia 2020-2021 (published 2026), referencing 2023 data.

titanium.name: UKTMP / ULBA Metallurgical Plant (Kazakhstan)
titanium.production: Kazakhstan national sponge metal production: 15,000 metric tonnes (2022), 14,000 metric tonnes (2023e), USGS MCS 2024. UKTMP (Ust-Kamenogorsk Titanium-Magnesium Plant) is Kazakhstan's primary titanium sponge producer.

titanium.name: Tronox Holdings plc
titanium.production: Annual titanium feedstock mining capacity approximately 832,000 metric tonnes (comprising 182,000 MT rutile/leucoxene, 240,000 MT synthetic rutile, 410,000 MT titanium slag) per Tronox 2025 10-K filing (SEC). Full year 2023 total revenue: $2,850 million (includes TiO2, zircon, pig iron, and other titanium products). TiO2 pigment not disaggregated from feedstock production in public reports.

titanium.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
titanium.production: 639,000 tonnes of zircon/rutile/synthetic rutile (Z/R/SR) total production in full year 2023. Source: Iluka Resources Quarterly Review to 31 December 2023 (January 23, 2024). Includes 211,000 tonnes of synthetic rutile sold in 2023.

titanium.name: Rio Tinto — Rio Tinto Iron & Titanium (RTIT)
titanium.production: Information not available — Rio Tinto's titanium operations (ilmenite, titanium slag, and pig iron from Sorel-Tracy, Quebec, Canada and Richards Bay Minerals in South Africa) are integrated. Exact 2023 titanium product tonnage not disaggregated in public annual reports.

titanium.name: Richards Bay Minerals (RBM) — Rio Tinto / Tronox joint venture
titanium.production: Information not available — RBM mines ilmenite, rutile, zircon, and leucoxene from heavy mineral sands at Richards Bay. Exact 2023 production not separately stated in public sources. Rio Tinto holds 74% of RBM.

titanium.name: Kenmare Resources plc — Moma Mine
titanium.production: Information not available — Kenmare operates the Moma Titanium Minerals Mine in Mozambique. 2023 annual production figures not extracted from primary source within research scope.

titanium.name: TIMET (Titanium Metals Corporation) — a Precision Castparts / Berkshire Hathaway company
titanium.production: Information not available — TIMET is one of the world's largest producers of titanium metal products (sponge, ingot, mill products). US sponge production is withheld (W) in USGS data; the Henderson, NV plant (capacity 12,600 t/yr) was idled since 2020 as of 2023; the Rowley, UT plant (capacity 10,900 t/yr) was idled since 2016. TIMET's Morgantown, PA and Toronto, OH facilities produce titanium mill products.

titanium.name: ATI Inc. (Allegheny Technologies Inc.)
titanium.production: Information not available — ATI operates the Rowley, Utah titanium sponge plant (capacity 10,900 t/yr, idled since 2016 per USGS). ATI produces titanium and specialty alloy mill products; no US sponge production in 2023.

uranium.name: Kazatomprom (National Atomic Company of Kazakhstan JSC)
uranium.production: 21,112 tonnes of uranium (tU) on a 100% basis in 2023; 11,169 tU on an attributable basis in 2023. Source: Kazatomprom FY2023 Operating and Financial Review (March 15, 2024) and FY2023 Financial Results (kazatomprom.kz, March 15, 2024). Kazakhstan national total: approximately 21,270 tU in 2023 per World Nuclear Association (2024); WNA 2024 data: 23,270 tU.

uranium.name: Cameco Corporation
uranium.production: 17.6 million pounds (approximately 7,983 tU) in 2023 (uranium segment production). Delivered 32 million pounds at an average realized price of $67.31 (CAD). Source: Cameco 2023 Annual Report (cameco.com) and Cameco 2023 Results announcement (February 8, 2024). WNA 2024 data for Cameco: 10,193 tU in 2024 (17% of world total).

uranium.name: Orano SA (formerly AREVA Mines)
uranium.production: Information not available — Orano's Mining Sector revenue was €1,319 million in 2023. Lower production volume at mining sites outside Canada noted in 2023 Annual Results (February 16, 2024). WNA 2024 data: 6,815 tU in 2024 (11% of world total). Orano's 2023 mines include Cigar Lake (Canada, joint with Cameco), Katco (Kazakhstan, joint with Kazatomprom), and previously Niger (Somaïr/Cominak, affected by coup).

uranium.name: Uranium One Inc. (subsidiary of Rosatom / ARMZ)
uranium.production: Information not available — Uranium One holds joint ventures with Kazatomprom in Kazakhstan (Karatau/Budenovskoye 2, Akdala, South Inkai 4, Khorassan 1) and other assets. WNA 2024 data: 5,829 tU in 2024 (10% of world total). 2023 production not separately disclosed.

uranium.name: JSC Atomredmetzoloto (ARMZ Uranium Holding — Rosatom)
uranium.production: 2,710 tonnes of uranium produced in 2023 (103% fulfillment of uranium production plan; 3% above target); up from 2,508 tU in 2022. Source: ARMZ Performance of the Mining Division 2023 (report.rosatom.ru/go_eng/2023/armz_2023_eng.pdf, 2024). Russia national total: approximately 2,738 tU in 2024 (WNA).

uranium.name: CGN (China General Nuclear Power Group) — Swakop Uranium (Husab Mine)
uranium.production: Information not available — Husab Mine (operated by Swakop Uranium, 75% CGN) produced approximately 4,437 tU in 2024 (WNA mine table). In 2023, Husab produced approximately 4,000-4,500 tU/yr (design capacity: ~6,000 tU/yr per World Nuclear Association/Oregon Group). Exact 2023 figure not available from primary company source.

uranium.name: Navoiyuran (Navoi Mining & Metallurgical Company — Uranium Division)
uranium.production: Approximately 4,000+ tU in 2023 (estimated: Uzbekistan produced 3,560 tU in 2022; 2023 production increased by 14% per Navoiyuran annual report 2023, implying ~4,059 tU). Production volume only reported in relative terms publicly; absolute 2023 figure not officially stated. WNA 2024 estimate for Uzbekistan: 4,000 tU.

uranium.name: BHP Group — Olympic Dam (uranium as co-product)
uranium.production: 3,406 metric tonnes of uranium produced in FY2023 (year ended 30 June 2023), up 43% from FY2022. Source: BHP Operational Review for the Year Ended 30 June 2023 (BHP, July 20, 2023). Uranium is a by-product of copper smelting at Olympic Dam.

uranium.name: Rössing Uranium Ltd. (majority owned by CNNC — China National Nuclear Corporation)
uranium.production: 2,920 metric tonnes of uranium oxide (tU equivalent) produced in 2023 (compared to 2,659 metric tonnes in 2022; 10% higher year-on-year). 9.3 million tonnes of ore milled. Source: Rössing Uranium Sustainability and Performance Report 2023 (rossing.com, launched June 18, 2024).

uranium.name: General Atomics / Quasar Resources (formerly AREVA Resources Australia — Beverley/Four Mile)
uranium.production: Information not available — Quasar Resources (General Atomics subsidiary) operates ISR uranium operations at Beverley North and Four Mile in South Australia. WNA 2024 data: General Atomics/Quasar: 1,808 tU in 2024 (3% of world total). 2023 production not separately stated.

uranium.name: Energy Fuels Inc.
uranium.production: 560,000 pounds (approximately 254 tU) of uranium sold from inventory in 2023 at an average realized price of $59.42/lb; sold from existing inventory (no new uranium produced from ore in 2023). Three mines commenced production in late December 2023. Source: Energy Fuels 2023 Annual Results (February 23, 2024, investors.energyfuels.com).

uranium.name: Paladin Energy Ltd.
uranium.production: Information not available — Langer Heinrich Mine in Namibia was under care and maintenance from 2018 until restart in March 2024; no production in 2023. By mid-2024 it had produced 235 tU during ramp-up. Source: World Nuclear Association, Namibia country profile (January 2026).

uranium.name: Uranium One / JV Budenovskoye LLP (Kazakhstan)
uranium.production: 3,299 tU from Karatau (Budenovskoye 2) mine in 2024 (WNA mine table); and 2,803 tU from Akdala & South Inkai 4 in 2024 (WNA). 2023 figures for individual joint ventures not separately available. Total Uranium One company production: 5,829 tU in 2024 (WNA).

rhodium.name: Anglo American Platinum (Amplats / Valterra Platinum)
rhodium.production: Rhodium 000 oz refined (Q3 2023 YTD): 164,300 oz; Q4 2023 refined: 61,300 oz; FY2023 total refined 'Other PGMs and gold' (incl. Rh, Ru, Ir, Au): 557,300 oz—no individual FY2023 rhodium annual total separately disclosed in Q4 2023 Production Report. Q4 2023 Production Report states total PGM (5E+Au) FY2023 refined production: 3,800,600 oz. Rhodium is included in the 5E+Au basket as one of five elements. Source: Anglo American Q4 2023 Production Report (angloamerican.com, dated 8 Feb 2024).

rhodium.name: Impala Platinum Holdings (Implats)
rhodium.production: Gross rhodium refined: 169,300 oz for financial year ended 30 June 2023 (FY2023). Source: Implats Summarised Consolidated Annual Results FY2023, SENS release 31 August 2023 (sharenet.co.za).

rhodium.name: Sibanye-Stillwater
rhodium.production: SA PGM operations 2023: 1,748,430 4Eoz (Pt+Pd+Rh+Au); US PGM underground 2023: 427,272 2Eoz (Pt+Pd). Rhodium for 2024 specifically reported as 190,000 oz (from company investor factsheet). Separate 2023 rhodium ounce figure not publicly broken out in 2023 annual reports; Sibanye is described as 'one of the world's largest primary producers of platinum, palladium, and rhodium'. Source: Sibanye-Stillwater Investor Factsheet (sibanyestillwater.com); Sibanye-Stillwater 2023 Integrated Report.

rhodium.name: Northam Platinum Holdings
rhodium.production: FY2023 (year ended 30 June 2023): Total equivalent refined metal produced from own operations: 892,876 oz 4E (Pt+Pd+Rh+Au). Individual rhodium ounce figure not separately disclosed in publicly available FY2023 reports; production reported in 4E aggregate. Source: Northam Platinum Holdings Annual Integrated Report FY2024 (Northam.co.za, FY2023 historical data shown).

rhodium.name: Zimplats Holdings (Impala subsidiary)
rhodium.production: FY2023 (year ended 30 June 2023): 6E production (Pt+Pd+Rh+Au+Ru+Ir) 611,000 oz. Separate rhodium figure not disclosed in FY2023 preliminary final report; rhodium revenue FY2023 US$169,279k vs FY2022 US$312,045k. Source: Zimplats ASX Preliminary Final Report FY2023 (zimplats.com, dated 29 Aug 2023).

rhodium.name: Nornickel (MMC Norilsk Nickel)
rhodium.production: Ranked No. 5 globally in rhodium production with approximately 8% market share (based on refined metal output), per Nornickel 2023 Annual Report PGM market analysis. Specific Nornickel rhodium tonnage/koz figure not separately published in consolidated production results. Source: Nornickel 2023 Annual Report (ar2023.nornickel.com); Johnson Matthey PGM Market Report 2024.

rhodium.name: Mimosa Mine (50% Impala Platinum / 50% Sibanye-Stillwater JV)
rhodium.production: FY2023: 6E production 245,000 oz in concentrate. Historical rhodium production (pre-2020): approximately 10,000 oz/year (Rhodium, koz: 10 in 2019 per miningdataonline.com). rhodium.name: Glencore (Integrated Nickel Operations, Sudbury)
rhodium.production: FY2023 from own sources (Integrated Nickel Operations, Sudbury, Canada): Rhodium 3,000 oz. Source: Glencore FY2023 Production Report (glencore.com, 1 Feb 2024).

rhodium.name: African Rainbow Minerals (ARM) — Two Rivers Platinum Mine
rhodium.production: Two Rivers Mine: Historical rhodium production (pre-2020): approximately 26,000 oz Rh/year (2019 data from miningdataonline.com). rhodium.production: US PGM underground operations 2023: 427,272 2Eoz (Pt+Pd). iridium.name: Anglo American Platinum (Amplats / Valterra Platinum)
iridium.production: Iridium is one of five elements in Amplats' 5E+Au basket (Pt+Pd+Rh+Ru+Ir+Au). No separate iridium production figure published for FY2023. iridium.production: Production data not publicly disclosed for iridium separately. FY2023 gross refined 6E: 2,958,700 oz; iridium is included in the 6E basket. iridium.production: Sibanye 'produces and refines iridium and ruthenium' per 2023 Integrated Report. 2024 iridium production reported as 64,000 oz on company investor factsheet. 2023 specific iridium figure not separately disclosed in 2023 annual reports. iridium.production: Production data not publicly disclosed for iridium separately. Northam reports production in 4E (Pt+Pd+Rh+Au) and 6E (4E + Ru + Ir) aggregate. iridium.production: Q2 FY2024 (quarter ended June 2023): Iridium 3,180 oz; Q1 FY2024 (quarter ended March 2023): Iridium 2,908 oz; Q2 FY2023 (quarter ended June 2022): Iridium 2,685 oz. germanium.name: Yunnan Lincang Xinyuan Germanium Industry Co., Ltd.

germanium.name: Teck Resources Limited

germanium.name: JSC Germanium (Germanium and Applications JSC)

germanium.name: 5N Plus Inc.

germanium.name: Nyrstar NV
germanium.production: Produces and recycles germanium commercially at European smelters; supplies germanium to 5N Plus; specific production volumes not publicly disclosed (source: USGS MCS 2024, Mining.com 2026)

germanium.name: Zinc Smelter (Clarksville, Tennessee) — Nyrstar

indium.name: Korea Zinc Co., Ltd.

indium.name: Various Chinese smelters (aggregate — China)

indium.name: Umicore S.A.

indium.name: Nyrstar NV (Auby smelter, France)

indium.name: Teck Resources Limited (Trail Operations)

indium.name: Zhuzhou Keneng New Material Co., Ltd.

tellurium.name: Various Chinese refineries (aggregate — China)

tellurium.name: Various Russian refineries (aggregate — Russia)

tellurium.name: Korea Zinc Co., Ltd.

tellurium.name: Zhuzhou Keneng New Material Co., Ltd.

hafnium.name: Western Zirconium Inc. (subsidiary of Westinghouse Electric Co.), Ogden, Utah

ferrochrome.production: Ferrochrome production capacity 530,000 tonnes per annum at Tornio Ferrochrome Works (three smelting furnaces). Actual 2023 ferrochrome output not separately disclosed; ~5% of global capacity per company.

ferrosilicon.name: Elkem ASA
ferrosilicon.production: Silicon-based alloys (ferrosilicon) shipments 191,431 MT in 2023 (FY2023 Annual Results). Leading producer of ferrosilicon (grades STD 75, STD 50) and specialty alloys at plants in Norway, Iceland, and other locations.

ferrosilicon.name: Finnfjord AS
ferrosilicon.production: Total ferrosilicon production capacity 100,000 tonnes per year (three electrical reduction furnaces). Actual 2023 production not publicly disclosed; 2019 production 86,994 MT ferrosilicon.

ferrosilicon.name: Ferroglobe PLC
ferrosilicon.country: United Kingdom (operations in USA, Spain, France, South Africa)
ferrosilicon.production: Silicon-based alloys (primarily ferrosilicon) shipments 191,431 MT in 2023 (FY2023 Annual Results). Annual capacity approximately 300,000 tonnes of silicon metal and silicon-based alloys combined.

ferrosilicon.name: Inner Mongolia Erdos Resources Co., Ltd.
ferrosilicon.production: World's largest ferrosilicon production base; annual ferrosilicon capacity of 800,000 metric tonnes (as of late 2025). Specific 2023 actual production not publicly disclosed in English.

ferrosilicon.name: Hoshine Silicon Industry Co., Ltd.
ferrosilicon.production: Major Chinese producer of industrial silicon and silicon-based materials. Specific ferrosilicon production tonnage not separately disclosed; total silicon revenue (TTM) CNY 21.53 billion.

ferrosilicon.name: JSC Chelyabinsk Electrometallurgical Plant (ChEMK)
ferrosilicon.production: Largest ferroalloy producer in Russia. Ferrosilicon production capacity approximately 500,000 tonnes per year; ferrochrome capacity ~250,000 tonnes per year. Specific 2023 actual production not publicly disclosed.

ferrosilicon.name: Mechel PAO
ferrosilicon.production: Ferrosilicon production at Bratsk Ferroalloy Plant. Quarterly ferrosilicon sales ~18,000 MT (Q1 2023). Specific 2023 full-year production not publicly disclosed in English.

ferrosilicon.name: PCC BakkiSilicon hf.
ferrosilicon.production: Annual silicon metal production capacity 35,000 metric tonnes per year (plant commissioned 2018, Húsavík). Note: Primarily silicon metal, not ferrosilicon. Actual 2023 production not publicly disclosed.

ferrosilicon.name: Companhia de Ferro Ligas da Bahia – FERBASA
ferrosilicon.production: Producer of ferrosilicon 75% (FeSi 75%) and ferrochrome in Brazil. Specific 2023 ferrosilicon production tonnage not publicly disclosed in English.

ferrosilicon.name: Samancor Chrome
ferrosilicon.production: Substantial producer of metallurgical ferrosilicon as a co-product of ferrochrome operations. Specific 2023 ferrosilicon production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

ferrosilicon.name: OM Holdings Ltd
ferrosilicon.production: Ferrosilicon production at Samalaju Industrial Park, Malaysia. Specific 2023 ferrosilicon production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

ferrosilicon.name: Sinosteel Jilin Ferroalloy Co., Ltd.
ferrosilicon.production: Major Chinese ferrosilicon producer. Specific 2023 production tonnage not publicly disclosed in English.

neodymium.name: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech Co., Ltd.
neodymium.production: World's largest rare earth producer based on Bayan Obo deposit. Produces separated neodymium oxide as a primary product (light rare earth focus). Plans to produce 161.4 kt REO in 2023 (per Asian Metal industry report). Specific neodymium-only production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: China Rare Earth Group Co., Ltd. (incl. China Southern Rare Earth Group)
neodymium.production: State-owned enterprise formed December 2021 controlling southern rare earth assets. Total 2023 first-batch smelting and separation quota 68,900 tonnes REO. Produces separated RE oxides including neodymium from ion-adsorption clay deposits; specific neodymium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: Shenghe Resources Holding Co., Ltd.
neodymium.production: Produces and trades rare earth concentrates, oxides, metals including neodymium. Rare earth segment revenue grew 26.9% in 2023. Specific neodymium production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: Xiamen Tungsten Co., Ltd. (XTC)
neodymium.production: Produces separated rare earth oxides including neodymium via smelting and separation operations in Fujian Province. Specific neodymium-only production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: MP Materials Corp.
neodymium.production: Produced 41,557 MT of rare earth oxides (REO) in concentrate in 2023; produced 200 MT of separated NdPr oxide in 2023 (commenced separation in 2023). Mountain Pass, California is only rare earth mining and processing site of scale in North America.

neodymium.name: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd.
neodymium.production: Total NdPr production 6,142 tonnes in FY2023 (FY2022: 5,880 tonnes); total REO production 16,780 tonnes in FY2023. Separation at Lynas Malaysia (Kuantan). Produces separated neodymium and praseodymium oxide.

neodymium.name: Neo Performance Materials Inc.
neodymium.production: Operates rare earth separation at Silmet facility (Estonia) and previously at Jiangyin (China, sold 2024). Produces separated RE oxides including neodymium. Specific neodymium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
neodymium.production: Building Australia's first fully integrated rare earth refinery at Eneabba, WA (expected operational ~2026; initial capacity 4,000 tpa REO, scaling to 5,500 tpa). Currently a monazite producer; separated neodymium production not yet at commercial scale.

neodymium.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
neodymium.production: Produces sintered neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) rare earth magnets using neodymium. Conducts rare earth separation and refinement for magnet production in Japan and Vietnam. Specific neodymium metal/oxide production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

neodymium.name: TDK Corporation
neodymium.production: Major NdFeB magnet manufacturer (NEOREC series); consumes neodymium (and praseodymium, dysprosium, terbium) in magnet production. Estimated NdFeB output in 8,000–12,000 tonne range. Specific neodymium consumption or separation not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech Co., Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Produces separated praseodymium oxide as a primary product (light rare earth focus). Typically reported together with neodymium as NdPr. Specific praseodymium-only tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: China Rare Earth Group Co., Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Produces separated RE oxides including praseodymium from ion-adsorption clay deposits in Jiangxi and Guangdong. Specific praseodymium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: Shenghe Resources Holding Co., Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Produces and trades rare earth products including praseodymium compounds and metals. Specific praseodymium production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: Xiamen Tungsten Co., Ltd. (XTC)
praseodymium.production: Produces separated rare earth oxides including praseodymium. Specific praseodymium-only production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: MP Materials Corp.
praseodymium.production: Produced 200 MT of separated NdPr oxide in 2023 (neodymium and praseodymium combined; not disclosed separately). Commenced NdPr separation in 2023.

praseodymium.name: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Total NdPr production 6,142 tonnes in FY2023 (neodymium and praseodymium combined; not disclosed separately). Separated at Lynas Malaysia.

praseodymium.name: Neo Performance Materials Inc.
praseodymium.production: Produces separated RE oxides including praseodymium at Silmet (Estonia) and formerly Jiangyin (China). Specific praseodymium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Eneabba rare earth refinery (under construction, expected ~2026) will produce separated praseodymium among other RE oxides. Commercial-scale separated production not yet commenced.

praseodymium.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
praseodymium.production: Produces sintered NdFeB magnets consuming praseodymium. Conducts integrated rare earth separation and refining for magnet manufacturing. Specific praseodymium production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

praseodymium.name: TDK Corporation
praseodymium.production: Consumes praseodymium in NdFeB magnet manufacturing (NEOREC series). Specific praseodymium consumption or separation not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: China Rare Earth Group Co., Ltd. (incl. China Southern Rare Earth Group)
dysprosium.production: Controls the primary global source of dysprosium from Jiangxi and Guangdong ion-adsorption clay deposits. China controls approximately 95% of global separation capacity for dysprosium. Specific dysprosium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech Co., Ltd.
dysprosium.production: Produces separated RE oxides including dysprosium, though primarily a light rare earth producer (Bayan Obo has limited heavy RE content). Specific dysprosium-only tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: Xiamen Tungsten Co., Ltd. (XTC)
dysprosium.production: Produces separated rare earth oxides including dysprosium from Fujian ion-adsorption clay operations. Specific dysprosium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: Shenghe Resources Holding Co., Ltd.
dysprosium.production: Produces separated RE oxides including dysprosium. Specific dysprosium production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd.
dysprosium.production: Commenced first production of separated dysprosium oxide at Lynas Malaysia in May 2025. As of FY2023, dysprosium separation was not yet operational. FY2023 dysprosium production: not applicable (pre-commissioning).

dysprosium.name: Neo Performance Materials Inc.
dysprosium.production: Commissioned heavy rare earth (HREE) solvent extraction line at Silmet (Estonia) in April 2026, producing separated dysprosium and terbium oxides. As of 2023, dysprosium separation at Silmet was pre-commissioning; Jiangyin (China) facility also produced dysprosium. Specific 2023 dysprosium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: MP Materials Corp.
dysprosium.production: Mountain Pass ore contains heavy rare earth elements including dysprosium, but separated dysprosium production not yet operational as of 2023. Specific dysprosium production: not applicable.

dysprosium.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
dysprosium.production: Consumes dysprosium in high-performance sintered NdFeB magnet manufacturing (for coercivity enhancement). Conducts rare earth separation and refining. Specific dysprosium production or consumption tonnage not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: TDK Corporation
dysprosium.production: Consumes dysprosium in NEOREC NdFeB magnets. Developing HAL technology to reduce dysprosium loading. Specific dysprosium consumption not publicly disclosed.

dysprosium.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
dysprosium.production: Eneabba rare earth refinery (under construction, expected ~2026) will produce separated dysprosium. Commercial-scale separated production not yet commenced as of 2023.

lanthanum.name: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech Co., Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Major producer of separated lanthanum oxide as a light rare earth from Bayan Obo. Plans to expand lanthanum and cerium production 25% by 2030. Specific 2023 lanthanum tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: China Rare Earth Group Co., Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Produces separated RE oxides including lanthanum. Specific lanthanum tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: Shenghe Resources Holding Co., Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Produces and trades rare earth products including lanthanum compounds. Specific lanthanum production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: Xiamen Tungsten Co., Ltd. (XTC)
lanthanum.production: Produces separated rare earth oxides including lanthanum. Specific lanthanum tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Produces lanthanum and cerium as co-products at Lynas Malaysia separation plant. FY2024 light rare earth sales approximately 8,500 t REO (lanthanum and cerium combined). FY2023 lanthanum-specific tonnage not separately disclosed; total REO production 16,780 t (FY2023).

lanthanum.name: MP Materials Corp.
lanthanum.production: Produces lanthanum in rare earth concentrate (41,557 MT REO in 2023). Separated lanthanum oxide production not specifically disclosed; NdPr is primary separated product as of 2023.

lanthanum.name: Neo Performance Materials Inc.
lanthanum.production: Produces separated RE oxides including lanthanum at Silmet (Estonia) and through its chemicals & oxides segment. Specific lanthanum tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Eneabba rare earth refinery (under construction, expected ~2026) will produce separated lanthanum among other RE oxides. Commercial-scale separated production not yet commenced as of 2023.

lanthanum.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
lanthanum.production: Produces separated rare earth materials including lanthanum compounds for electronic and industrial applications via high-purification technology. Specific lanthanum production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

lanthanum.name: TDK Corporation
lanthanum.production: Consumes lanthanum in specialty materials and electronic components. Specific lanthanum production or consumption tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: China Rare Earth Group Co., Ltd. (incl. China Southern Rare Earth Group)
terbium.production: Controls the primary global source of terbium from Jiangxi and Guangdong ion-adsorption clay deposits. China controls approximately 95% of global separation capacity for terbium. Specific terbium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech Co., Ltd.
terbium.production: Produces separated RE oxides including terbium; plans 40% expansion in dysprosium and terbium separation capacity through 2030. Primary production is light rare earths; terbium-specific tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: Xiamen Tungsten Co., Ltd. (XTC)
terbium.production: Produces separated rare earth oxides including terbium from Fujian operations. Specific terbium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: Shenghe Resources Holding Co., Ltd.
terbium.production: Produces and trades rare earth products including terbium compounds. Specific terbium production tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: Neo Performance Materials Inc. (Silmet, Estonia)
terbium.production: Commissioned heavy rare earth (HREE) solvent extraction line at Silmet facility (Estonia) in April 2026, producing separated terbium and dysprosium oxides at nameplate capacity. As of 2023, Silmet terbium separation was pre-commissioning; JAMR (China) also produced terbium. Specific 2023 terbium tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd.
terbium.production: Commenced first production of separated terbium oxide at Lynas Malaysia in June 2025 (initial commissioning of HRE facility). As of FY2023, terbium separation was not yet operational. FY2023 terbium production: not applicable.

terbium.name: MP Materials Corp.
terbium.production: Mountain Pass ore contains terbium, but separated terbium production not yet operational as of 2023. Specific terbium production: not applicable.

terbium.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
terbium.production: Consumes terbium in high-performance sintered NdFeB magnet manufacturing (grain boundary diffusion for coercivity). Conducts integrated rare earth refining. Specific terbium production or consumption tonnage not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: TDK Corporation
terbium.production: Consumes terbium in NEOREC NdFeB magnets. Developing HAL technology to reduce heavy RE (Dy, Tb) loading. Specific terbium consumption not publicly disclosed.

terbium.name: Iluka Resources Ltd.
terbium.production: Eneabba rare earth refinery (under construction, expected ~2026) will produce separated terbium. Commercial-scale separated production not yet commenced as of 2023.
